sourceSummary.oneLine: Cybersecurity risk management report for H&abyz G/GT series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models, including GT1717C and GT1717CP wired-only derivatives.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports technical documentation by documenting cybersecurity risk analysis, evaluation, controls, residual risk acceptability, and production/post-production monitoring for the G/GT series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
keyPoints: The report states it was prepared in accordance with ISO 14971:2019 and TR 24971:2020.
The product is indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children and is not to be used for mammography.
The GT series wired detector is described as a derivative of the G series, with WiFi-related threats and controls not applicable.
A total of 67 cybersecurity risks were identified through STRIDE threat modeling.
Initial evaluation found 43 acceptable risks and 24 unacceptable risks; after controls, all 67 residual risks were reported as acceptable.
Production and post-production activities include annual cybersecurity risk review, CISA catalog verification, complaint handling, design-change review, and adverse event reporting if necessary.

evidence.claim: The document is A-CRMR-BLU, Rev. No. 1, dated April 14, 2026.
evidence.evidenceText: No. A-CRMR-BLU Rev. No. 1 Cybersecurity Risk Management Report Rev. Date April 14, 2026

evidence.claim: The report covers G series and GT series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models.
evidence.evidenceText: G series Detector G1417CW, G1417CWP, G1417FW, G1417FWP, G1717CW, G1717CWP, G1717FW, G1717FWP GT series Detector GT1717C, GT1717CP
evidence.pageTarget: G/GT Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The report revision was made due to addition of wired-only derivative models GT1717C and GT1717CP.
evidence.evidenceText: Revision due to the addition of wired-only derivative models (GT1717C, GT1717CP).

evidence.claim: The intended use is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: The G/GT series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The device does not include the X-ray generator or console application software.
evidence.evidenceText: does not include the X-ray generator or console application software.

evidence.claim: A cybersecurity-compliant console application must be used with the device.
evidence.evidenceText: (A cybersecurity-compliant console application must be used)

evidence.claim: GT series devices are wired derivatives of the G series and WiFi-related threats and controls do not apply to the GT series.
evidence.evidenceText: The wired GT series detector is a derivative of the G series, with wireless features and other auxiliary functions removed. Therefore, cybersecurity testing and related documentation were primarily conducted and prepared based on the G series. For the GT series, WiFi-related threats and controls are not applicable.

evidence.claim: The report identifies 67 risks through STRIDE threat modeling.
evidence.evidenceText: A total of 67 risks were identified through STRIDE Threat modeling.

evidence.claim: The SBOM vulnerability review found no publicly known vulnerabilities affecting the product.
evidence.evidenceText: After reviewing NIST’s NVD and the CISA KEV Catalog for all off‑the‑shelf and open‑source components in the SBOM, no publicly known vulnerabilities affecting this product were identified.

evidence.claim: CVSS version 3.1 was used for cybersecurity vulnerability evaluation.
evidence.evidenceText: NVD – CVSS c3.1 Calculator / CVSS version 3.1

evidence.claim: Initial risk evaluation classified 43 risks as acceptable and 24 risks as unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Green zone, Acceptable Risk 43 Red zone, Unacceptable Risk 24 Total 67

evidence.claim: After risk controls, all 67 identified residual risks were acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Green zone, Acceptable Risk 67 Red zone, Unacceptable Risk - Total 67

evidence.claim: No further additional risk control measures were needed after residual risk evaluation.
evidence.evidenceText: All identified residual risks evaluated after implementation of the risk control measures, are distributed in a broadly acceptable region. So, there are no further additional risk control measures needed.

evidence.claim: Cyber risks are to be reviewed once a year and the product updated if risk is identified.
evidence.evidenceText: cyber risks to the product will be reviewed once a year to update the product if there is a risk.

evidence.claim: Customer cybersecurity issues are handled through change management and service processes.
evidence.evidenceText: In the event of a cyber security issue from a customer, communicate with the customer according to the change management procedure and service process.

evidence.claim: The quality manager reports safety information and side effects to the regulatory agency when necessary.
evidence.evidenceText: the Quality manager shall report safety information and any side effects to the regulatory agency (Applicable national or regional regulations).

reviewFlags.reason: Authority or jurisdiction is not explicitly named in the source text; source metadata indicates MFDS/domestic but the document text only refers to applicable national or regional regulations.
Potential conflicting arithmetic in risk severity statement: the document states 67 risks were assessed, then states 59 were High and 13 were Medium, which totals 72.
OCR or text extraction quality issues are present, including broken words and apparent typos such as 'metho ds', 'Treat modeling', 'waning', and 'CISC monitoring'.
The source text is marked confidential and intended solely for H&abyz Co., Ltd.
